structured_fields.name: investigational_drugs
structured_fields.description: Investigational drugs currently in clinical trials — each with key trial properties

example.modality: Biologic — type I interferon receptor antagonist

example.indication: SLE with skin and joint involvement

structured_fields.name: phase_distribution
structured_fields.description: Count of investigational drugs per clinical trial phase — used for bar chart rendering

freetext_fields.name: novel_modalities_narrative
freetext_fields.description: Narrative covering emerging therapeutic modalities in the pipeline — gene therapy, CAR-T, biologics, small molecules, RNA-based therapies. Explain what makes each approach novel and what clinical problem it addresses.

freetext_fields.name: companies_partnerships_narrative
freetext_fields.description: Narrative on key companies driving the pipeline and notable partnerships, licensing deals or collaborations shaping the competitive landscape

freetext_fields.name: clinical_trial_landscape_narrative
freetext_fields.description: Narrative on the overall clinical trial landscape — trial design trends, patient recruitment challenges, endpoints being used and regulatory considerations

charts.title: Pipeline Distribution by Clinical Phase
charts.caption: Number of investigational drugs per clinical trial phase

prompt_hints.tone: forward-looking and analytically grounded — balance optimism with clinical realism
avoid: listing drugs without clinical or mechanistic context
overstating pipeline maturity without phase evidence
inventing trial data — if uncertain mark status as unconfirmed
ignoring failed or discontinued trials where relevant
prefer: connecting investigational mechanisms to unmet needs already established
noting where pipeline drugs represent genuine mechanistic innovation
acknowledging competitive dynamics between companies where relevant
phase distribution counts that are consistent with the drug list provided
